---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

```{python}
#import sys
#!{sys.executable} -m pip install requests
# %%
# The usuals
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```

Use the flights dataset from the past

```{python}
df = pd.read_json("https://github.com/byuidatascience/data4missing/raw/master/data-raw/flights_missing/flights_missing.json")

df = df.replace({"n/a": np.nan,
                 "1500+": 1500,
                -999: np.nan,
                "unknown": np.nan})

df.num_of_delays_carrier = df.num_of_delays_carrier.astype(int)

fillvalues = {'num_of_delays_late_aircraft': df.num_of_delays_late_aircraft.mean(),
              'minutes_delayed_carrier': df.minutes_delayed_carrier.mean(),
              'minutes_delayed_nas': df.minutes_delayed_nas.mean()}


df_cleaned = df.fillna(value = fillvalues)
df_cleaned
```

# Dataset partition

Now the dataset is clean, I want to split it into test/train.

```{python}
#get our inputs by dropping categorical variables, and those correlated at 100% with the target
x = df_cleaned.drop(columns = ['airport_code', 'airport_name', 'month'])
y = df_cleaned.airport_code

# print(x.shape)
# print(y.shape)
```


```{python}
import sklearn
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(x, y, test_size = .3, random_state = 17)
# print(X_train.shape, y_train.shape)
# print(X_test.shape, y_test.shape)
```

Fit the model

```{python}
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier #import the model
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

# clf = DecisionTreeClassifier() #defines the model
clf = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators = 400)

clf.fit(X_train, y_train)
```

Use the model to make predictions and evaluate its performance

```{python}
pred = clf.predict(X_test)

from sklearn.metrics import classification_report

print(classification_report(y_test, pred))
```

# Task 3, looking at feature importances


```{python}
influences = pd.DataFrame({'importance': clf.feaure_importances_,
    'feature': X_train.columns})
influences
```


```{python}
(
ggplot(data = influences,
        mapping = aes(x = 'feature', y = 'importance')) +
        geom_bar(stat = 'identity') +
        coord_flip()
)
```

# Stretch part 3, regression model

Trying to predict a continuous value (a number), instead of a category

```{python}
x = df_cleaned.drop(columns = ['airport_code', 'airport_name', 'month', 'minutes_delayed_total', 'year'])
y = df_cleaned.minutes_delayed_total
```


```{python}
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(x, y, test_size = .2)
```


```{python}
from sklearn import linear_model
model = linear_model.Ridge()
model.fit(X_train, y_train)
```

Evaluating the model performance


```{python}
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error, mean_absolute_error, r2_score

preds = model.predict(X_test)
mse = mean_squared_error(y_test, preds)
mae = mean_absolute_error(y_test, preds)
rsquared = r2_score(y_test, preds)

print(f"The mse is {mse}")
print(f"The mae is {mae}")
print(f"The R squared is {rsquared}")
```

NOT REQUIRED FOR THIS ASSIGNMENT
```{python}
residuals = y_test - preds

df_resid = pd.DataFrame({'ytest': y_test,
                        'preds': preds,
                        'resids': residuals})
df_resid

```


```{python}
residuals vs. predicted
(
    ggplot(data = df_resid) + 
    geom_point(aes(x = 'preds', y = 'resids'))
    
)
```
